title: Identifying angles of elevation and depression
problem: A person stands $15$ m from the base of a tree and looks up at the top at an angle of $40°$ above the horizontal. (a) Name the type of angle formed. (b) Identify which side of the right-angled triangle represents the height of the tree, relative to the $40°$ angle.

steps.instruction: Determine whether the person is looking up or down from horizontal.
steps.working: The person is looking upward from the horizontal, so the angle is an angle of elevation.

steps.instruction: Sketch the right-angled triangle. The $40°$ angle sits at the observer's position (on the ground). Label the three sides.
steps.working: $$\text{hypotenuse} = \text{line of sight}, \quad \text{adjacent} = 15 \text{ m (horizontal base)}, \quad \text{opposite} = h \text{ (height of tree)}$$

steps.instruction: Identify the height of the tree relative to the $40°$ angle.
steps.working: The height of the tree is directly across from the $40°$ angle, so it is the opposite side.

steps.instruction: Set up the ratio to find the height (for reference).

answer: The angle is an angle of elevation. The height of the tree is the side opposite the $40°$ angle.

title: Finding a height using angle of elevation
problem: From a point $40$ m from the base of a vertical tower, the angle of elevation to the top is $38°$. Find the height of the tower, correct to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Identify the known information and the unknown.
Choose the trigonometric ratio. The adjacent side ($40$ m) and opposite side ($h$) are involved, so use TOA.
Evaluate using a calculator and round to 2 decimal places.

title: Multi-step: distance between two boats from a cliff
problem: From the top of a cliff $60$ m above sea level, the angles of depression to two boats directly out to sea are $25°$ (farther boat) and $40°$ (nearer boat). Find the distance between the two boats, correct to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Set up the diagram. The cliff is $60$ m high. Two lines of sight go to the two boats. Using alternate interior angles, the angle at the base of each right-angled triangle equals the angle of depression.
Find the horizontal distance from the cliff base to the nearer boat.
Find the horizontal distance from the cliff base to the farther boat.
Subtract to find the distance between the two boats.

answer: The distance between the two boats is approximately $57.16$ m.
